content.title: MAGE-RAG: Multigranular Adaptive Graph Evidence for Agentic Multimodal RAG in Long-Document QA
content.summary: MAGE-RAG introduces a graph-based agentic RAG framework that adaptively constructs evidence subgraphs across multiple granularities for long multimodal documents, achieving 52.75% accuracy on LongDocURL and 53.26% on MMLongBench-Doc.
content.summary_1: Long-document multimodal question answering requires integrating evidence from text, tables, images, charts, and complex layouts scattered across lengthy PDFs. MAGE-RAG solves this with an offline evidence graph (page + element nodes) and an online evidence controller that iteratively activates, searches, and prunes evidence under explicit budgets. Benchmarks show MAGE-RAG outperforms Direct MLLM, Text RAG, and Page-level Visual RAG approaches on both LongDocURL and MMLongBench-Doc.
content.body_text: Long-document multimodal QA requires a system to locate sparse evidence in long PDFs and integrate clues from text, tables, images, charts, and complex layouts. Existing RAG methods mostly rely on fixed Top-k retrieval over text chunks or pages. MAGE-RAG builds an offline evidence graph with page nodes and element nodes encoding containment, reading order, layout adjacency, section hierarchy, and semantic-neighbor relations. At query time, an online evidence controller iteratively activates, searches, and prunes evidence under explicit budgets. Results: 52.75 overall accuracy on LongDocURL, 53.26 accuracy with 51.19 F1 on MMLongBench-Doc. Code: https://github.com/laonuo2004/MAGE-RAG

tech_insights.impact_summary: Graph-based agentic RAG that dynamically constructs evidence subgraphs sets new benchmarks for long multimodal document understanding.
actions_developer: Replace static Top-k RAG with graph-based evidence subgraph construction
Use offline graph pre-build + online query-time pruning for cost-effective multimodal retrieval
Test on enterprise document workflows (legal, financial, medical PDF corpora)
actions_business: Evaluate for document intelligence workflows requiring multimodal understanding
Consider for insurance, legal, and research document Q&A systems
Benchmark against current PDF-RAG solutions to measure accuracy improvement
